Panama Restoration Lab

Bioregional Observatory/4 Bioregional Organizations/Panama Restoration Lab.md
Activities
33314415-10e1-81e1-a7e3-c17751e3828e, 33314415-10e1-813c-b738-c4869386f6bf, 33314415-10e1-8175-8f1a-d95adcc1e4bb
type
organization
location_lat
9
background_section
## Background Panama packs exceptional biological diversity into a small territory—forests, wetlands, grasslands, and soils that once buffered hydrology and livelihoods. Those systems are now stressed: rainfall shifts, weakened infiltration, and canal operations exposed to water risk. A severe drought recently throttled the **Panama Canal**, costing [billions of dollars](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nowoYmBRijw) in trade disruption. Climate is part of the story; land use is another—deforestation, erosive farming, and poor water governance reduce local rainmaking and storage. Functional ecosystems regulate moisture; degraded ones amplify scarcity. The **Panama Restoration Lab** exists to show how deliberate restoration can tie landscape repair to drought relief, canal resilience, and community well-being—an argument pitched at local, national, and global audiences. ### Origins and Network The Lab frames restoration as multi-scale infrastructure: neighborhood and farm benefits; **Panama Canal** watershed security; and an exportable template for climate adaptation through regenerative land use. Staff and allies combine restoration science, policy, and finance. **EcoRestoration Alliance** supplies technical depth; **Biodiversity for a Livable Climate** carries education and advocacy; **CCT Foundation** anchors Panama-based coordination; **ForTerra Panama** pilots commercial soil and reforestation pathways, including carbon revenues to recycle into work on the ground. Together they form the Lab’s joint platform for implementation, monitoring, and learning.
Sources

activities
La Arenosa Pilot Site|Forest Regeneration and Cattle Ranching|Panama Venture Studio + Restoration Fund
description
The Panama Restoration Lab is an emerging field-building effort working to demonstrate that ecological restoration can serve as a strategic tool for climate resilience, drought mitigation, and livelihood regeneration—not only for Panama, but as a model for the world.
id
panama-restoration-lab
vision_legacy_section
## Vision and Legacy The Lab has stood up the farm pilot and is deepening ties with communities and agencies. Next steps sketched include more sites across the **canal** basin and **Azuero** Peninsula, stronger hydrologic evidence, a restoration **center of excellence**, and global dissemination—showing that landscape repair can stabilise both water for shipping and wellbeing for people. The ambition parallels Panama’s historic canal role: once a chokepoint for trade, now potentially a reference for **climate** and **stewardship** infrastructure.
website
https://panamarestoration.org/
bioregional_context
Panama's global relevance stems from its ecological importance and economic centrality via the Panama Canal, where a historic drought recently disrupted trade and exposed deep vulnerabilities in water systems.
category
Bioregional Organizer
